title: Google Photorealistic 3D Basemap Now Available in ArcGIS Online
take: Photorealistic 3D scenes in StoryMaps will change how community presentations feel — policy problems become tangible when you can walk through a neighborhood in 3D.

title: The 15-Minute City: Network Analysis vs. Euclidean Distance in Walkability Scoring
take: Using road network distance instead of straight-line radius makes walkability scores far more accurate — I need to update the scoring in my village simulator.

title: Place-Based GIS Education Increases Spatial Retention by 38% in Middle School Students
take: Connecting GIS to students' own neighborhoods — not abstract datasets — is the core finding, and it validates everything we've been doing with the 4-H Geospatial curriculum in Wake County.

title: Food Desert Identification and Grocery Access Gaps in North Carolina's Rural Counties
take: 11 Wake County census tracts meet the USDA food desert definition — the methodology here maps directly onto the NOURISH project and gives us a defensible framework for advocacy.

title: AI-Assisted Crop Disease Classification from Drone Imagery at 94% Accuracy
take: The training pipeline they used for strawberry leaf disease detection is essentially what we built in Granja Node — worth exploring transfer learning from their model weights.
